text: Dev teams avoiding responsibility and pushing work onto PM/PO

Another pricing page that wants me to 'talk to sales' - I need to see actual costs per user so I can budget for the whole cross-functional team, not sit through a demo where they'll try to upsell enterprise features we don't need.

These testimonials all sound like marketing speak. Show me actual engineering teams using this for design system maintenance, not PMs talking about 'improved communication' - I need proof this won't become another tool we abandon after three months.

This navigation is already confusing me and I'm the one evaluating it. If I can't figure out basic functionality quickly, there's no way my designers and engineers will adopt this without constant support tickets.

Why do they need my team size, company info, and project details just to start a trial? This feels like they're more interested in their sales pipeline than letting me test if their tool actually works with our workflow.

Finally, a tool that starts with technical integration docs instead of team personality assessments. The API documentation looks solid and they have clear versioning - this might actually solve our design system governance problems.
